# Release v1.1.2

## Summary

`v1.1.2` is a reliability-focused patch release. It hardens the single-owner
transport path so transient socket and lock failures recover cleanly, enriches
`subagent.ended` with a normalized `data.endReason` field for downstream
consumers, and makes local release validation match the GitHub publish lane
more closely.

## Highlights

- Improved owner transport recovery so a transient relay socket bind failure no
  longer requires restarting the gateway runtime before follower events can
  flow again.
- Added normalized `subagent.ended.data.endReason` values:
  `completed`, `deleted`, `swept`, `released`, and `unknown`.
- Hardened release engineering with a tracked release script, pinned release
  lane via `.nvmrc`, shared verification commands, and compatibility CI across
  Node 20, 22, and 24.

## Transport Reliability And Diagnostics

- Owner-intended runtimes now retry transport recovery after transient relay
  socket startup failures instead of silently remaining followers.
- Stale transport locks are reclaimed immediately when the recorded owner PID is
  already dead, even if the lock heartbeat timestamp still looks fresh.
- Transport logging now includes structured recovery context such as:
  `runtimeId`, `transportMode`, `role`, `socketPath`, `lockPath`,
  `pendingEvents`, `reason`, and `error`.
- Followers keep queued relay events while owner recovery is pending, improving
  recovery behavior after temporary local transport disruption.
- Added regression coverage for:
  - owner demotion and recovery after socket bind failures
  - dead-PID lock reclamation
  - actionable relay failure logging
  - owner relay server listening diagnostics

## Event Contract Improvements

- `subagent.ended` now exposes a stable `data.endReason` field so consumers can
  branch on a canonical termination reason instead of runtime-specific payload
  shapes.
- Older OpenClaw runtimes that do not provide a reason now emit
  `data.endReason: "unknown"` instead of omitting the field.
- Canonical event generation now preserves the normalized `endReason` even if
  passthrough payload data contains a conflicting value.
- Added dedicated unit and integration coverage for the normalization path and
  backward-compatibility fallback behavior.

## Tool Hook Runtime Behavior

- `tool_result_persist` registration now uses the fire-and-forget typed hook
  helper so it keeps the intended synchronous registration behavior while still
  logging failures safely through the shared hook wrapper.

## Release And CI Improvements

- Added a tracked `scripts/release.sh` flow so release behavior is versioned
  with the repository instead of drifting locally.
- Added `.nvmrc` and a pinned release lane so local releases can intentionally
  match the GitHub publish environment.
- Added shared verification commands:
  - `npm run verify:release`
  - `npm run verify:ci`
- Updated the publish workflow to use the shared verification command and the
  repository-pinned Node version.
- Updated CI to exercise compatibility on Node 20, 22, and 24 while keeping the
  publish lane pinned for deterministic npm releases.
- Added release and testing docs covering the new flow and parity guidance.

## Breaking Changes

- None.

## Upgrade Notes

- No config migration is required.
- Downstream consumers may safely start reading `subagent.ended.data.endReason`.
  Existing consumers that ignore the new field remain compatible.
- For release work, use the repo-pinned toolchain before tagging:
  - `nvm use`
  - `npm run verify:ci`

## Validation

- Reviewed all commits and diffs since `v1.1.1`.
- Verified release preflight with:
  - `npm run verify:ci`
- Validation currently covers:
  - 40 test suites
  - 264 passing tests
  - coverage thresholds satisfied
